personal.metaDescription: Niharika Kumari — PhD Research Scholar at NIT Durgapur working at the intersection of biotechnology, environmental sciences, and human health using network biology and bibliometric analysis.
personal.metaKeywords: Niharika Kumari, PhD, NIT Durgapur, Biotechnology, Bioprocess, Environmental Sciences, Network Biology, VOSviewer, RStudio, Research Scholar

about.biography:
I am a doctoral candidate at the National Institute of Technology Durgapur, specialising in biotechnology and bioprocess research. My academic journey spans some of India's finest institutions — from Banaras Hindu University, where I built my foundation in botany and plant biology, to Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi, where I deepened my understanding of environmental sciences.

My current doctoral research explores the intersection of environmental stressors, biological processes, and human health — a theme that demands rigorous analytical tools alongside innovative experimental approaches. I am proficient in VOSviewer for bibliometric and knowledge-mapping analysis, Network Biology methods for understanding complex biological interactions, and RStudio for statistical modelling and data visualisation.

I am driven by the belief that the most meaningful scientific advances emerge from interdisciplinary collaboration. I actively seek academic partnerships and funding-driven research opportunities that address pressing environmental and biological challenges with tangible translational impact.
about.researchVision: My vision is to decode how environmental stressors perturb biological networks and accelerate disease — connecting molecular-level mechanisms to population-scale health outcomes. By integrating experimental biology with computational tools, I aim to generate actionable insights that inform both policy and therapeutic strategies.
about.researchPhilosophy: Rigorous science, open collaboration, and translational intent. I believe that meaningful research must speak across disciplines — drawing from botany, environmental science, and biotechnology simultaneously — and must ultimately serve human and ecological wellbeing.
researchObjectives: Investigate the molecular mechanisms by which environmental stressors disrupt biological processes
Apply network biology approaches to map stress-response pathways in living systems
Use bibliometric analysis (VOSviewer) to identify research gaps and emerging trends in biotechnology
Develop bioprocess strategies that harness biological responses to environmental stimuli
Build interdisciplinary frameworks linking environmental science data with human health outcomes
about.currentWork: Currently pursuing my doctoral thesis at the Department of Biotechnology, NIT Durgapur, focused on the bioprocess and biological dimensions of environmental stress. I am actively leveraging VOSviewer for systematic literature mapping, RStudio for multivariate data analysis, and network biology tools to model stress-response interactions.

researchInterests.title: Biotechnology & Bioprocess Engineering
researchInterests.description: Exploring biological systems and bioprocess strategies that can be harnessed or engineered for industrial, medical, and environmental applications, with a focus on stress-response pathways.

researchInterests.title: Environmental Sciences & Stressors
researchInterests.description: Investigating how environmental stressors — including pollutants, climate change variables, and ecological disruptions — interact with biological systems at molecular and organismal levels.

researchInterests.title: Network Biology
researchInterests.description: Applying network science approaches to model biological interaction networks, identify key regulatory nodes, and understand how perturbations propagate through living systems.

researchInterests.title: Bibliometrics & Knowledge Mapping
researchInterests.description: Using tools like VOSviewer to perform systematic bibliometric analysis, identify research trends, map collaboration networks, and uncover emerging frontiers in biotechnology and environmental research.

researchInterests.title: Environment–Health Interface
researchInterests.description: Studying the mechanisms through which environmental exposures translate into human health outcomes, bridging environmental biology with biomedical sciences for translational applications.

researchInterests.title: Plant Biology & Botany
researchInterests.description: Drawing from a foundational background in botany and plant biology to understand plant responses to environmental stressors and their applications in bioremediation and sustainable biotechnology.

publications.title: Bibliometric Analysis of Environmental Stress Research in Biotechnology: Trends, Gaps, and Future Directions
publications.abstract: A comprehensive bibliometric analysis using VOSviewer mapping the global research landscape of environmental stress in biotechnology, identifying key research clusters, collaboration networks, and emerging thematic frontiers.

publications.title: Network Biology Approaches to Understanding Environmental Stress-Response Pathways
publications.abstract: Application of network biology tools to construct and analyse stress-response interaction networks, identifying key regulatory nodes and their biological relevance in environmental stress contexts.

publications.title: Environmental Stressors and Human Health: A Systematic Review and Pathway Analysis
publications.abstract: A systematic review synthesising current evidence on molecular mechanisms connecting environmental stressors to human health outcomes, with a focus on biological pathway perturbations and translational implications.

projects.title: Bibliometric Mapping of Biotechnology & Environmental Stress Research
projects.shortDescription: Systematic VOSviewer-based bibliometric analysis to map the global knowledge landscape of environmental stress in biotechnology.
projects.description: This project applies VOSviewer and bibliometric methods to analyse thousands of publications from Scopus and Web of Science, constructing co-citation networks, keyword co-occurrence maps, and institutional collaboration networks. The aim is to identify research gaps, emerging trends, and key contributors in the biotechnology–environmental stress domain.
projects.methodology: Database search and data extraction from Scopus/WoS; data cleaning and normalisation; VOSviewer network construction (co-citation, keyword co-occurrence, bibliographic coupling); trend analysis in R.
projects.results: Identified major research clusters and knowledge gaps; mapped top contributing countries, institutions, and journals; manuscript in preparation.

projects.title: Network Biology Analysis of Environmental Stress-Response Pathways
projects.shortDescription: Construction and analysis of biological interaction networks to understand how environmental stressors perturb cellular pathways.
projects.description: Using protein-protein interaction databases (STRING, BioGRID) and gene expression data, this project constructs and analyses biological networks underlying stress-response mechanisms. Network topology metrics identify hub genes and critical regulatory nodes relevant to environmental stress.
projects.methodology: Network construction from curated interaction databases; topology analysis (degree, betweenness centrality, clustering coefficient); GO and pathway enrichment analysis; identification of hub and bottleneck nodes.
projects.results: Identified key regulatory hubs in stress-response networks; enrichment analysis revealed stress-responsive biological processes; results being prepared for publication.

projects.title: Environmental Stressors and Human Health: Systematic Review
projects.shortDescription: A comprehensive review synthesising molecular and epidemiological evidence linking environmental exposures to health outcomes.
projects.description: A PRISMA-guided systematic review analysing literature on the biological mechanisms through which environmental stressors — including air pollutants, heavy metals, and climate-related exposures — affect human health at cellular and molecular levels.
projects.methodology: PRISMA-guided systematic literature search; PICO framework; data extraction and quality assessment; narrative and quantitative synthesis; RStudio for statistical summaries.
projects.results: Synthesised evidence from 80+ primary studies; identified 5 major mechanistic pathways; manuscript under review.

blogs.title: Why Bibliometrics Matters: Using VOSviewer to Navigate the Ocean of Scientific Literature
blogs.excerpt: The volume of scientific publications doubles every nine years. Bibliometric tools like VOSviewer offer researchers a compass — mapping knowledge landscapes, revealing hidden clusters, and pointing to the whitespace where new discoveries wait.

blogs.title: Network Biology: Seeing Life as a System, Not Just a Sum of Parts
blogs.excerpt: Every cell is a city, and every protein a citizen with relationships. Network biology gives us the map. Here's how thinking in networks is transforming our understanding of environmental stress and disease.

blogs.title: From Botany to Bioprocess: My Interdisciplinary PhD Journey
blogs.excerpt: Starting with leaves and roots in Varanasi, moving through environmental sciences in Delhi, and now diving into biotechnology in Durgapur — here is what I have learnt about following curiosity across disciplines.

testimonials.name: PhD Supervisor
testimonials.quote: Niharika demonstrates a strong aptitude for interdisciplinary research, combining analytical rigour with genuine intellectual curiosity. Her proficiency with bibliometric and network biology tools brings a distinctive computational perspective to our laboratory.

testimonials.name: Research Colleague
testimonials.quote: Working alongside Niharika has been genuinely enriching. Her enthusiasm for bridging environmental science with biotechnology, and her willingness to collaborate openly, makes her an invaluable part of our research community.
